sn45.21:1.2: “Wrong way” is micchattā, literally “wrongness”. | The roots of both sammā (Sanskrit samyak) and micchā (Sanskrit mithyā) convey a similar sense of “together”, but sammā implied harmonious union (Rig Veda 4.58.6, 5.9.5, 9.73.2), while micchā was associated with contention or clashing (1.119.3, 1.144.3, 6.25.2). Over time, micchā came to convey “wrong, false, vain, mistaken”, while sammā was “right, harmonious, integrated”.
